name: S&P 500
summary: Day 72 Sunday (markets closed; Friday May 8 close still operative). **MAY 8 TAPE: TWO SIMULTANEOUS REGIMES COEXISTING. CRISIS-DECOUPLED ASSETS RUN AI SUPER-CYCLE INDEPENDENTLY: S&P 500 7,398.93 (+0.84% 1D NEW RECORD), NASDAQ COMPOSITE 26,247.08 (+1.7% 1D, +15.0% 1M), SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M, +39.3% 3M); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+0.1% 1D, +36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M — single largest 30-day move in any major index in recent memory); MSCI EM ETF $67.94 (+2.0% 1D, +12.7% 1M); NIKKEI -0.2% 1D BUT +18.1% 1M; TAIWAN TAIEX +19.7% 1M; VIX 17.08-17.19 CYCLE-LOW.** Crisis-driven assets continue to price the trinary regime: Brent close $101.73 (+1.66% 1D, intraday range $96-104); Heating Oil +2.2% 1D / +61.3% 3M; RBOB Gasoline +2.0% 1D / +77.6% 3M (cleanest cost-of-living transmission); Frontline +3.6% 1D / +13.5% 1M; BOAT +2.3% 1D. **Defense complex actively unwinding: LOCKHEED $506.51 (-1.2% 1D, -18.8% 1M, -20.2% 3M) — cleanest 'ceasefire takes the war premium out' signal in the cycle, despite May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + CENTCOM 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech.** Markets choosing diplomatic-track narrative over trinary-regime operational reality. Either (a) right that deal signs by May 14, or (b) setup for sharp reversal if Iran rejects past May 21 (StanChart $130-145 path). **Cheniere -2.7% 1D, -9.7% 1M** = $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices); CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players are SHORT the war via hedge books. **CF Industries -3.1% 1D** on Q1 EPS $3.98 (vs $2.50 est) blowout — sell-the-news after +54% YTD run; fertilizer cascade trade tired at this level despite operational tailwind. KOSPI +36.5% MoM is THE most reversible position in the world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June. Bonds: US 10Y 4.36% (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M); 2Y 3.60%; DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M). Stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print reduces immediate Fed-cut probability for June.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — MAY 8 CLOSES OPERATIVE: S&P 500 7,398.93 RECORD (+0.84% 1D, +8.4% 1M); NASDAQ 26,247.08 (+1.7% 1D, +15.0% 1M); SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M); VIX 17.19 cycle-low. **Two days of unsettled news flow accumulated against frozen prices** — Sunday bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike (NEW, first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5, FIRST inside Qatari EEZ), CENTCOM precision tally 58/4 (UPDATED), Qatar PM Miami trip + Axios Qatar-IRGC scoop (UPDATED-MAJOR), Bahrain 41 IRGC detentions (NEW), Kharg satellite slick (NEW), UAE air-defense scale refined (UPDATED). **Monday May 11 Asian open will be first re-pricing event.** Markets pricing Branch 3 (de-escalation) for risk assets, Branch 2 (trinary regime) for energy, Branch 1 (kinetic) for nobody — most internally inconsistent positioning of cycle. Defense complex actively unwinding (LMT $506.51 -1.2% 1D, -18.8% 1M, -20.2% 3M); Cheniere -2.7% 1D on $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM; CF Industries -3.1% on Q1 EPS blowout (sell-the-news). **VIX 17.19 cycle-low is the most aggressive 'war doesn't matter for equities' trade since 2022 Russia-Ukraine post-shock period — if Iran rejects past May 21, VIX could spike to 28-32 in 2-3 sessions. Asymmetric long-vol setup of cycle.** **KOSPI +36.5% MoM is THE most reversible position in world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June (~5 weeks remaining).**
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED multi-source price tape (May 8 close); accumulated unpriced news = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO/CENTCOM/Axios/Bahrain MoI/Copernicus/UAE Defense Ministry primaries)
